d:personname.d:surname: Joint Chiefs of Staff Pub 2.0: Appendix B - Intelligence Disciplines - http://www.dtic.mil/doctrine/new_pubs/jp2_0.pdf IC-CIO

d:info.d:title: RR-SM Schematron Guide
d:info.d:subtitle: Guide to Schematron Rules and Patterns

d:author.d:orgname: Prepared by the Joint Chiefs of Staff Pub 2.0: Appendix B - Intelligence Disciplines - http://www.dtic.mil/doctrine/new_pubs/jp2_0.pdf IC-CIO

d:info.d:address: Distribution Notice: This document has been approved for Public Release and is available for use without restriction.
d:info.d:releaseinfo: This document has been approved for Public Release by the Office of the Director of National Intelligence. See 'Distribution Notice' for details.

d:sect1.d:title: Purpose
d:sect1.d:para: (U) The following documentation is informative. The actual Schematron files are the normative record. This documentation is generated from the Schematron files via XSLT it may be missing some file or pieces of a file but whatever is here other than titles came from the original file.
(U) It is envisioned that this will be a useful resource to search and read but for questions and debates the source files should be consulted.

(U) Rules identifiers are all of the format RR-SM-ID-XXXXX, with rule files named RR-SM_ID_XXXXX.sch. Any other heading indicates a supporting file that may strongly influence a rule but is not actually a numbered rule.

d:chapter.d:title: Rules
d:chapter.d:para: All of the numbered Rules for RR-SM are listed in this section. These rules may depend strongly on patterns defined in the Abstract Patterns section or on variables defined in the Schematron Schema section.

d:sect1.d:title: .//Rules/GeneralConstraints/RR-SM_ID_00001.sch
d:sect1.d:para:
Rule Description: RR-SM-ID-00001
[RR-SM-ID-00001][Error] The @ism:DESVersion is less than the minimum version allowed: 9.
Human Readable: The ism version imported by RR-SM must be greater than or equal to 9.
Code Description: For all elements that contain @ism:DESVersion, we verify that the version is greater than or equal to the minimum allowed version: 9.

Schematron Code:<?ICEA pattern?><!-- Notices - Distribution Notice: This document has been approved for Public Release and is available for use without restriction. --> <sch:pattern id="RR-SM-ID-00001">
<sch:rule context="*[@ism:DESVersion]"> <sch:assert test="number(@ism:DESVersion) >= 9" flag="error"> [RR-SM-ID-00001][Error] The @ism:DESVersion is less than the minimum version allowed: 9.
Human Readable: The ism version imported by RR-SM must be greater than or equal to 9. </sch:assert> </sch:rule> </sch:pattern>

d:sect1.d:title: .//Rules/GeneralConstraints/RR-SM_ID_00002.sch
d:sect1.d:para:
Rule Description: RR-SM-ID-00002
[RR-SM-ID-00002][Error] The @arh:DESVersion is less than the minimum version allowed: 1.
Human Readable: The arh version imported by RR-SM must be greater than or equal to 1.
Code Description: For all elements that contain @arh:DESVersion, we verify that the version is greater than or equal to the minimum allowed version: 1.

Schematron Code:<?ICEA pattern?><!-- Notices - Distribution Notice: This document has been approved for Public Release and is available for use without restriction. --> <sch:pattern id="RR-SM-ID-00002">
<sch:rule context="*[@arh:DESVersion]"> <sch:assert test="number(@arh:DESVersion) >= 1" flag="error"> [RR-SM-ID-00002][Error] The @arh:DESVersion is less than the minimum version allowed: 1.
Human Readable: The arh version imported by RR-SM must be greater than or equal to 1. </sch:assert> </sch:rule> </sch:pattern>

d:chapter.d:title: Abstract Patterns
d:chapter.d:para: All of the Abstract Patterns for RR-SM are listed in this section. These patterns may depend strongly on variables defined in the Schematron Schema section.

d:chapter.d:title: Schematron Schema
d:chapter.d:para: The top level Schematron file for RR-SM is in this section. This file imports all of the others and also defines many global variables they are all dependent on.

d:sect1.d:title: .//RR-SM_XML.sch
d:sect1.d:para:
Schematron Code:<?ICEA master?><!-- UNCLASSIFIED --><!-- Notices - Distribution Notice: This document is being made available by the Intelligence Community Chief Information Officer to Federal, State, Local, Tribal, and Foreign Partners and associated contractors. Approval for any further distribution must be coordinated via the Intelligence Community Chief Information Officer, Mission Engagement Division at standardssupport@dni.gov--><!-- WARNING: Once compiled into an XSLT the result will be the aggregate classification of all the CVES and included .sch files --> <sch:schema xmlns:xsl="http://www.w3.org/1999/XSL/Transform" queryBinding="xslt2"> <sch:ns uri="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" prefix="xlink"/> <sch:ns uri="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" prefix="xs"/> <sch:ns uri="urn:us:gov:ic:ism" prefix="ism"/> <sch:ns uri="urn:us:gov:ic:arh" prefix="arh"/>
<!--****************************--> <!-- (U) RR-SM ID Rules --> <!--****************************-->
<!--(U) GeneralConstraints--> <sch:include href="./Rules/GeneralConstraints/RR-SM_ID_00001.sch"/> <sch:include href="./Rules/GeneralConstraints/RR-SM_ID_00002.sch"/> </sch:schema>

d:chapter.d:title: Removed Rules
d:chapter.d:para: All of the numbered Rules for RR-SM that have been removed are listed in this section. This section is just a reference for what rule numbers have been dropped. In many but not all cases there will be a reason listed. In all cases the version that the rule was dropped in is listed.
